title: Indra festival duration
claim_or_observation: Festival is announced for twenty-eight days
notes: Verify against original Tamil and multiple editions

title: Civic renewal during festival
claim_or_observation: Old sand is removed, fresh sand spread, and public spaces decorated
notes: Requires line-level citation

title: Festival and public welfare
claim_or_observation: Festival is linked to rain, prosperity, and protection from famine, sickness and enmity
notes: Do not flatten into later ritual categories

title: Plural teachers and debate
claim_or_observation: Teachers of multiple paths and philosophers gather for public debate
notes: Check terminology in Tamil

title: Multilingual port population
claim_or_observation: Visitors and speakers of many languages participate in the city
notes: Cross-check Pattinappalai

title: Aputhiran challenges divine prestige
claim_or_observation: Feeding suffering beings is placed above reward in Indra's heaven
notes: Important for interpretation of Indra

title: Poompuhar at Kaveri-sea confluence
claim_or_observation: Poompuhar is identified as a Sangam-age Chola port at the river-sea confluence
notes: Capture access date and archive link

title: Poompuhar excavations
claim_or_observation: Excavations are reported at Kizharveli and Dharmakulam
notes: Consult full excavation reports

title: Underwater evidence limitation
claim_or_observation: Underwater remains must not be presented as proof of every literary claim
notes: Binding publicity and production rule

title: Puhar design language
claim_or_observation: Puhar is reconstructed as a timber-brick-plaster river-sea port rather than a later medieval temple city
notes: Requires specialist material-culture review

title: Saduvan storm and ship separation
claim_or_observation: Saduvan travels by ship, encounters a storm, is separated from the vessel and reaches an island
notes: Verify wording and mechanism against original Tamil

title: Ethics of rescuing shipwreck survivors
claim_or_observation: Saduvan instructs the island community to save people who reach shore after ships break and sink
notes: Central ethical use of maritime danger

title: Shipwreck cargo categories
claim_or_observation: Goods associated with sailors and wrecks include sandalwood, jewels and soft cloth
notes: Not a complete cargo manifest

title: Merchant voyage to Manipallavam
claim_or_observation: Nine merchants travel by ship with Aputhiran and return after realizing he was abandoned
notes: Supports organized merchant travel and return navigation

title: Kizharveli wharf interpretation
claim_or_observation: Brick walls, platforms and wooden poles are interpreted as a wharf of approximately the fourth century CE
notes: Full excavation report required before detailed reconstruction

title: Offshore stone masonry
claim_or_observation: Stone masonry was reported near Vanagiri and tentatively dated between the second century BCE and fourth century CE
notes: Purpose and chronology require specialist review

title: Alagankulam long-distance exchange
claim_or_observation: Excavations yielded Mediterranean-region pottery, amphora fragments, rouletted ware, Tamil-Brahmi sherds, beads and Roman coins
notes: Comparative Tamil coastal evidence; not transferable item-for-item to Puhar

title: Sewn-plank Indian Ocean tradition
claim_or_observation: Sewn-plank vessels are widely documented in Indian Ocean maritime scholarship across long periods
notes: Does not prove a specific Puhar hull or date

title: Poompuhar later wreck exclusion
source_work: Project methodology based on archaeological reports
claim_or_observation: The cannon-and-lead-ingot wreck dated to the 1790s is not evidence for Manimekalai-period ship design
notes: Binding production and publicity rule

title: Distributed Puhar harbour model
claim_or_observation: Puhar is provisionally reconstructed with river landing, wharf, estuary anchorage, offshore transfer and warehouse zones
notes: Requires palaeochannel, harbour and nautical-archaeology review
